title: Thibault's legacy recalled
description: PLETTENBERG BAY NEWS AND VIDEO - Louis...
bodyText:
[{"type":"paragraph","text":"Video","html":"Video"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"PLETTENBERG BAY NEWS AND VIDEO - Louis Michel Thibault, considered the father of South African architecture, left a legacy in South Africa that few others can lay claim to.","html":"PLETTENBERG BAY NEWS AND VIDEO - Louis Michel Thibault, considered the father of South African architecture, left a legacy in South Africa that few others can lay claim to."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"He was the creator of what was to become known as the Cape Dutch style, according to fellow architect and Plett resident Rodney Grosskopff."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Grosskopff himself has an architectural quiver to his bow - he was involved in the design of the round Ponte building in Hillbrow, Johannesburg.","html":"Grosskopff himself has an architectural quiver to his bow - he was involved in the design of the round Ponte building in Hillbrow, Johannesburg."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Grosskopff paid tribute to Thibault's \"truly South African style\" at a presentation to the Van Plettenberg Historical Society (VPHS) on Friday 12 September at the Plettenberg Hotel.","html":"Grosskopff paid tribute to Thibault's \"truly South African style\" at a presentation to the Van Plettenberg Historical Society (VPHS) on Friday 12 September at the Plettenberg Hotel."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"BirthplaceAmong Thibault's legacy is the Drostdy in Graaff-Reinet built in 1804, which is still in use as a five-star hotel.","html":"Birthplace
Among Thibault's legacy is the Drostdy in Graaff-Reinet built in 1804, which is still in use as a five-star hotel."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Groot Constantia's homestead also reflects his work – he was definitely responsible for the wine cellar, while Anton Anreith contributed to the celebrated pediment.","html":"Groot Constantia's homestead also reflects his work – he was definitely responsible for the wine cellar, while Anton Anreith contributed to the celebrated pediment."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Born in 1750 in Picquigny, France, a small town on the River Somme, near Amien, Thibault arrived in the Cape in 1783 as part of a team of Swiss army mercenaries employed by the VOC (Dutch East India Company)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"After peace was declared, he remained in the Cape and set up a private architectural practice in collaboration with Anton Anreith, a young sculptor and woodcarver from Freiburg, and Hermann Schutte, a young architect and builder from Bremen.","html":"After peace was declared, he remained in the Cape and set up a private architectural practice in collaboration with Anton Anreith, a young sculptor and woodcarver from Freiburg, and Hermann Schutte, a young architect and builder from Bremen."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Sadly there is no information about where in South Africa he was buried, but his mastery can still be viewed at the Lodge of Good Hope in Cape Town.","html":"Sadly there is no information about where in South Africa he was buried, but his mastery can still be viewed at the Lodge of Good Hope in Cape Town."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Video:"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Keeping up tradition: Van Plettenberg Historical Society chairperson Len Swimmer (left) presents a penguin statue to keynote speaker Rodney Grosskopff. \"Penguin\" rhymes with \"Picquigny\", the birthplace of Louis Michel Thibault, quipped Swimmer. Photo: Chris van Gass","html":"Keeping up tradition: Van Plettenberg Historical Society chairperson Len Swimmer (left) presents a penguin statue to keynote speaker Rodney Grosskopff. \"Penguin\" rhymes with \"Picquigny\", the birthplace of Louis Michel Thibault, quipped Swimmer. Photo: Chris van Gass"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Read previous article:","html":"Read previous article:"},{"type":"list","items":["Thibault's architectural legacy uncovered"],"ordered":false},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]
